definition: The retailization of private credit, with $200B+ in semi-liquid vehicles held primarily by 55+ HNW investors offering quarterly liquidity windows against illiquid underlying loans, creates an untested liquidity risk channel with potential for gate-contagion cascades.
evidence: $200B+ in semi-liquid vehicles (interval funds, non-traded BDCs)
Quarterly liquidity windows against illiquid underlying loans (5–7 year WAL)
Gate-contagion scenario: rational individually, catastrophic in aggregate
open_questions: When and under what macro conditions the aging cohort will draw down
Whether fund gates would trigger broader contagion
